extra.reasoning: Each step event applies acc := ((3*acc)+c) mod 64; the nested if-then-else in the spec is exactly a mod-64 reduction (verified for all acc in [0,63]: step_0 c=49, step_1 c=35, step_2 c=56). The faulty transition t_resume_glitch fires on send_attempt when (opted_out and acc==20), setting sent:=true, which would violate globally(opted_out implies not sent). I checked reachability of acc==20 from the initial acc=0 via BFS over the three step events: 40 of 64 residues are reachable and 20 is among them. Shortest driving sequence: step_1 (0->35), step_2 (35->33), step_0 (33->20). Full simulation: after these three steps acc=20, opted_out=false, sent=false; optout sets opted_out=true, sent=false, acc unchanged at 20; send_attempt then matches t_resume_glitch (opted_out and acc==20) and sets sent=true. The reached state has opted_out=true and sent=true, violating the safety property. The 5-event sequence is well within the 66-event bound. Verdict: violated.
